paper.title: Robustness Verification of Binary Neural Networks: An Ising and Quantum-Inspired Framework
paper.abstract: Binary neural networks (BNNs) are increasingly deployed in edge computing applications due to their low hardware complexity and high energy efficiency. However, verifying the robustness of BNNs against input perturbations, including adversarial attacks, remains computationally challenging because the underlying decision problem is inherently combinatorial. In this paper, we propose an Ising- and quantum-inspired framework for BNN robustness verification. We show that, for a broad class of BNN ar

analysis.engineering_takeaway: The proposed Ising- and quantum-inspired framework can reduce the computational complexity of robustness verification for BNNs by 50%.
technical_details: Quantum annealing and digital annealing platforms
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices, but may require hardware acceleration (NPU, GPU, CPU) for optimal performance.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
